catalogue.definition: Identification of the ADS-B station providing information.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    - The up-to-date list of SACs is published on the EUROCONTROL
      ASTERIX Web Site
      (http://www.eurocontrol.int/services/system-area-code-list).
catalogue.title: Data Source Identification

catalogue.definition: Characteristics of the originating ADS-B unit.

values: Highly manoeuvrable (5g acceleration capability) and high speed (>400 knots cruise)

catalogue.title: Emitter Category

catalogue.definition: Time of applicability (measurement) of the reported position, in the form of elapsed time since last midnight, expressed as UTC.
catalogue.remark: The time of the day value is reset to zero at every midnight.
catalogue.title: Time of Day

catalogue.definition: The maximum difference between the actual time of applicability of the reported position and the time reported in the Time of Day item (I021/030).
catalogue.title: Time of Day Accuracy

catalogue.definition: Type and characteristics of the data as transmitted by a system.

values: Equipment capable to provide Selected Altitude
Equipment not capable to provide Selected Altitude

catalogue.title: Target Report Descriptor

catalogue.definition: Mode-3/A code converted into octal representation.
catalogue.remark:
Notes:

    1. Bit 15 (G) is set to one when an error correction has been attempted.
    2. Bit 16 (V) is normally set to zero, but can exceptionally be set to one
    to indicate a non-validated Mode-3/A code (e.g. alert condition detected,
    but new Mode-3/A code not successfully extracted)

values: Mode-3/A code derived during last update
Mode-3/A code not extracted during the last update

catalogue.title: Mode 3/A Code in Octal Representation

catalogue.definition: Target address (emitter identifier) assigned uniquely to each target.
catalogue.title: Target Address

catalogue.definition: ADS figure of merit (FOM) provided by the aircraft avionics.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    bits-4/1 (PA) code the “Navigational Uncertainty Categories –
    Position” as described in the ADS-B MASPS [Ref. 3]
catalogue.title: Figure of Merit

catalogue.definition: Velocity uncertainty category of the least accurate velocity
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    bits-8/1 code the “Navigational Uncertainty Categories – Velocity”
    as described in the ADS-B MASPS [Ref. 3]
catalogue.title: Velocity Accuracy

catalogue.definition: Reports indicating the 4D intended trajectory of the aircraft.
catalogue.remark:
Notes:

    1. NC is set to one when the aircraft will not fly the path described
       by the TCP data.
    2. TCP numbers start from zero.
    3. LT = Lateral Type
    4. VT = Vertical Type
    5. TOV gives the estimated time before reaching the point. It is
       defined as the absolute time from midnight.
    6. TOV is meaningful only if TOA is set to 1.

values: Trajectory Intent Data is available for this aircraft
Trajectory Intent Data is not available for this aircraft
Trajectory Intent Data is not valid

catalogue.title: Trajectory Intent

catalogue.definition: Calculated Position in WGS-84 Co-ordinates with a resolution of 180/(2^25) degrees.
catalogue.remark:
Notes:

    1. Positive longitude indicates East. Positive latitude indicates North.
    2. The LSB provides a resolution at least better than 0.6m.
catalogue.title: Position in WGS-84 Co-ordinates

catalogue.definition: Relative strength of received signal.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    The value is implementation-dependent, 0 being the minimum detectable
    level for that system.
catalogue.title: Signal Amplitude

catalogue.definition:
Vertical distance between the target and the projection of its position
on the earth’s ellipsoid, as defined by WGS84, in two’s complement form.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    1. LSB is required to be less than 10 ft by ICAO.
catalogue.title: Geometric Altitude

catalogue.definition:
Flight Level from barometric measurements,not QNH corrected, in two’s
complement form.
catalogue.title: Flight Level

catalogue.definition:
The short-term vertical intent as described by either the FMS selected
altitude, the Altitude Control Panel Selected Altitude, or the current
aircraft altitude according to the aircraft's mode of flight.
catalogue.title: Intermediate State Selected Altitude

catalogue.definition:
The vertical intent value that corresponds with the ATC cleared altitude,
as derived from the Altitude Control Panel (MCP/FCU).
catalogue.title: Final State Selected Altitude

catalogue.definition: Calculated Air Speed (Element of Air Vector).
catalogue.title: Air Speed

catalogue.definition: True Air Speed.
catalogue.title: True Airspeed

catalogue.definition: Magnetic Heading (Element of Air Vector).
catalogue.title: Magnetic Heading

catalogue.definition: Barometric Vertical Rate, in two’s complement form.
catalogue.title: Barometric Vertical Rate

catalogue.definition: Geometric Vertical Rate, in two’s complement form, with reference to WGS-84.
catalogue.title: Geometric Vertical Rate

catalogue.definition: Ground Speed and Track Angle elements of Ground Vector.

items.name: GS
items.title: Ground Speed in Two's Complement Form Referenced to WGS84

catalogue.title: Ground Vector

catalogue.definition: Rate of Turn, in two’s complement form.
catalogue.remark:
Notes:
    1. A positive value represents a right turn, whereas a negative value
       represents a left turn.
    2. Value 15 means 15 °/s or above.
catalogue.title: Rate Of Turn

catalogue.definition:
Target (aircraft or vehicle) identification in 8 characters, as reported
by the target.
catalogue.title: Target Identification

catalogue.definition: Status of the target
catalogue.title: Target Status

catalogue.definition:
Indication of which ADS link technology has been used to send the target
report.
catalogue.title: Link Technology Indicator

catalogue.definition: Meteorological information.
catalogue.title: Met Information

catalogue.definition: The roll angle, in two’s complement form, of an aircraft executing a turn.
catalogue.remark:
Notes:
    1. Negative Value indicates “Left Wing Down”.
    2. Resolution provided by the technology “1090 MHz Extended Squitter”
       is 1 degree.
catalogue.title: Roll Angle

catalogue.definition: Expansion
catalogue.name: RE
catalogue.title: Reserved Expansion Field

catalogue.definition: Special Purpose Field
catalogue.name: SP
catalogue.title: Special Purpose Field

preamble:
Surveillance data exchange.
ADS-B Target Reports.
title: ADS-B Target Reports
